title: Grants Pass Teen Era
html:
The 2018-2020 stretch in Grants Pass is the most concentrated formation period in the corpus — Yahir between roughly 16 and 18, a Mexican-American kid in a small southern Oregon town, doing fifteen things at once and quietly laying down the operating system he'd be running a decade later. The notes from this era do not look like a teenager's notes. They look like a junior analyst's day-planner that occasionally remembers it also has wrestling practice and a Hamilton paper due.

The substrate is everything everywhere all at once: FBA competition prep, AP US History reviews, Huck Finn, Chinese class recordings, wrestling, Costco contacts for an Amazon FBA hustle, Brenda mentor conversations, Renaissance + Ford + OSU + JCEF scholarship apps in parallel, Sun Tzu's Art of War on loan to a friend, and a transcribed Pokémon type-effectiveness chart on Christmas Eve 2018 titled "Normal > Neutral to Everything" — the most efficient self-portrait in the entire corpus. The teen era is where the matrix-thinking instinct first appears, where titles already mismatch contents (a chronic Yahir tic), and where he writes the line "Improving the human condition — what is the human condition?" at age 16 (2019 11 13 coding zeal).

The macro brain is also here, fully formed. By March 2020 — eleven days into the COVID crash — he writes a five-line thesis framing the pandemic as "an exogenous event, like 9/11, already underlined with ropes conditions today, like the dot com bubble" (2020 03 24 exogenous event 911). A month later he is quoting Charlie Munger ("Go where they ain't", "What's within your circle of competence?"), tracking Beyond Meat, Planet Fitness, and Trade Desk on a custodial TDA account, prepping the mini-pet-mart job interview, and writing "Gain competitive advantage by learning about ai and making useful for all / Think beginning of internet with aol and computers only for spreadsheets" — the seed of rankett hiding in a 17-year-old's to-do dump (2020 04 23 go to diamond). The "SEO enhancement" income idea from 2020 11 15 scholarship essays todo is the same instinct six years before AI visibility became a category.

Three other patterns crystallize here. First, the family-translator role — ghostwriting his mother Iracema Vargas's emails to school admin (2021 05 30 iracema graduation letter), brokering hard topics on behalf of Emilio, proposing his dad onto DoorDash. Second, the interview-people-you-want-to-learn-from template ("Questions for Raissa: Would you do it again / Did you miss your family") that he'd reuse verbatim in 2024 Jensen prep and 2026 rankett meetups. Third, the firm-friend-accountability voice ("you need to dial in… please face whatever void") drafted to Dave in 2021 — already the same tone he'd use in the 2024 04 27 sam messages.

The era closes with the Ford full-ride hitting and the Egypt entry on a 2021 bucket list that he'd actually visit by 2023 — proof of concept for the manifestation pattern formalized in 2023 12 29 vision for big life.

Source summaries

  • 2018 11 21 voice memo 1339 — earliest preserved voice-memo recording (transcript noise only)
  • 2018 12 24 normal neutral to everything
  • 2019 03 14 voice memo 1625 — broker investing-101 conversation at 15
